subject: English
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the science fiction setting of the story and life in a space station
Analyze the role of Claribel the canary in helping detect danger
Explain the sequence of events that led the crew to discover the air problem
Understand how observation and scientific knowledge can solve real-life problems
Identify important vocabulary and expressions used in the story
Develop reading comprehension, reasoning, and discussion skills

lesson_plan.title: Pre-Reading Discussion: Feathered Friends
topics_covered: Meaning of feathered and furry friends
student_activities: Explain which bird they like and why

lesson_plan.title: Setting of the Story: Life in Space
topics_covered: Work of construction crews in space
Introduction to Sven Olsen and Claribel
teaching_strategy: Guided reading of the first section of the story
student_activities: Discuss how work in space is different from Earth

lesson_plan.title: Claribel in the Space Station
topics_covered: Sven’s curiosity about bird flight in space
student_activities: Discuss why Sven wanted to observe the bird

lesson_plan.title: The Turning Point in the Story
topics_covered: Connection with canaries used in coal mines
teaching_strategy: Teacher explanation of the key event
Discussion linking science with the story
student_activities: Explain how the narrator remembered miners using canaries
Discuss how animals can warn humans about danger

lesson_plan.title: Discovery of the Problem and Resolution
topics_covered: Importance of safety measures in space
student_activities: Create a cause-and-effect chain of events
Explain how Claribel indirectly saved the crew

lesson_plan.title: Language and Skill Development
topics_covered: Merged words such as brunch, smog, and vlog
student_activities: Match words to form merged words
Write sentences using future tense forms

teaching_aids: Pictures or videos of space stations
Chart showing canaries used in coal mines
teacher_tip: Encourage students to connect the story with real science. Discuss how miners historically used canaries to detect poisonous gases and how a similar idea appears in the story.
